notes: Realism-style: bank-owned trust company offering RIA services, with an affiliated trust company (parent), affiliated registered fund complex, and uses third-party model providers (sub-advisers). No soft dollars, no directed brokerage. Tests the 'affiliated_bank' field which is rare in our other fixtures, the proprietary-fund-recommendation conflict, and the 'uses_other_investment_advisers' flag.

inputs.item_10_body:
Item 10 — Other Financial Industry Activities and Affiliations

The firm is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Cornerstone Trust Company, N.A., a national banking association chartered under the laws of the United States and regulated by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. Cornerstone Trust serves as custodian for substantially all client assets managed by the firm.

The firm serves as the investment adviser to the Cornerstone Funds, a family of registered open-end investment companies registered with the SEC under the Investment Company Act of 1940. The firm receives investment management fees from the Cornerstone Funds in accordance with the management agreement between the firm and each Fund and as disclosed in each Fund's prospectus and statement of additional information. The firm has a financial incentive to recommend the Cornerstone Funds to its advisory clients because such recommendations result in additional management fees to the firm. The firm addresses this conflict by (i) considering both Cornerstone Funds and unaffiliated alternatives in client portfolio construction, (ii) crediting back to client accounts the portion of the Cornerstone Funds management fees attributable to advisory client investments where required to avoid double-charging, and (iii) requiring an annual review of all proprietary product recommendations by the firm's Investment Committee.

The firm has no affiliated broker-dealer, insurance agency, or other investment adviser. The firm engages unaffiliated third-party investment advisers as model portfolio providers and as sub-advisers for certain client accounts; selection and monitoring of such third parties is conducted pursuant to a written due-diligence process and the firm pays the relevant fees out of its own resources rather than charging clients separately.
inputs.item_12_body:
Item 12 — Brokerage Practices

Client assets managed by the firm are held in custody at Cornerstone Trust Company, N.A., the firm's affiliated bank. Trade execution for client accounts is conducted through Cornerstone Trust's institutional trading desk, which routes orders to a network of executing broker-dealers selected by Cornerstone Trust based on best-execution criteria.

The firm does not directly select broker-dealers for client transactions and does not enter into soft-dollar arrangements with any broker-dealer. The firm does not receive research or brokerage services in exchange for client commissions.

Clients are not permitted to direct brokerage to a particular broker-dealer; all transactions for client accounts are executed through Cornerstone Trust's trading desk as described above.

The firm aggregates client orders for the same security when consistent with its duty of best execution and its trade-allocation policy. Allocations among participating client accounts are made on a pro-rata basis subject to the firm's written allocation policy.
